hero.title: Sora 2 Pro
hero.intro: Longer, production-ready clips from text or images — native voice + ambience with enhanced prompt control.

overview: Generate longer AI videos with sound and scene control using Sora 2 Pro. Perfect for storytelling, explainers, and branded motion design—starting from image or text prompts.

custom.heroSupportLine: Cleaner detail, steadier motion, more reliable multi-beat continuity.
custom.heroTrustLine: Pay-as-you-go · Price shown before you generate
custom.heroDesc1: Sora 2 Pro is the premium sibling to Sora 2. The current MaxVideoAI route exposes 720p/1080p output, explicit audio control, and richer prompt chaining for multi-beat sequences.
custom.heroDesc2: Prototype fast with Sora 2, then move into Sora 2 Pro to regenerate your winning shots with tighter control over timing, motion, and sound.
heroHighlights: High-res modes for cleaner detail||Sharper textures and more polished frames for client-ready deliverables.
Synced audio built-in||Dialogue + sound effects land better when you add 1–2 deliberate audio cues.
Stronger results with structured prompts||Shot lists and clear beats execute more reliably than “adjective soup.”
Best for final takes||Use it when the concept is locked and you want the cleanest output.

items: Prefer structured shot lists over long prose.

items: Call out ambience vs dialogue explicitly.

Mute and finish in post when needed.

custom.specValueProp: Sora 2 Pro is for polished 720p/1080p outputs with audio control and multi-scene continuity on the current MaxVideoAI route—ideal once your Sora 2 explorations are locked.
custom.imageIntro: Lock style with a still, then animate with precise motion and audio cues.
imageFlow: Create or import a branded keyframe (style locked).
Send it to Sora 2 Pro as Image → Video.
Describe motion per beat and any sound you need (VO, ambience, FX).
Regenerate to polish timing while preserving your look.
imageWhy: Campaign intros that must stay on-brand
custom.multishotTitle: Multi-Shot & Sequenced Clips in Sora 2 Pro
custom.multishotIntro1: Chain 2–3 scenes with explicit timing to keep continuity tight.
custom.multishotIntro2: Use the audio toggle and cues to align voice-over, FX, and music hits.
multishotTips: Cap at 3 scenes for clarity in 12 seconds.
Repeat core descriptors (outfit, setting, lighting) per scene.
Specify camera per beat: push-in, crane up, handheld glide.
Write the transition in the prompt (match cut, whip pan, crossfade).
strengths: 720p/1080p delivery for premium outputs on the current MaxVideoAI route
Audio toggle with explicit cue handling
Great for ads, trailers, explainers, client decks
Pairs with Sora 2 for prototype → Pro finalize
boundaries: Still no long-form editing; stitch multiple renders
Needs clear continuity notes to avoid drift
Tiny on-screen text can remain challenging
safetyRules: No real people, public figures, minors, hateful or violent content.
Do not use likeness without consent.
Sensitive prompts or references may be blocked.
safetyInterpretation: Generic characters and scenes are fine.
Famous people or sensitive topics can be filtered.
custom.safetyNote: Stay within safe use to keep Sora 2 Pro reliable for production work.
comparisonPoints: Prototype in Sora 2 (720p) for speed and cost efficiency.
Finalize in Sora 2 Pro (720p/1080p on MaxVideoAI today) for polish, audio control, and continuity.
Same workflow: swap engines in the same GUI when you’re ready to upscale.

faqs.q: Does Sora 2 Pro include audio control?
faqs.a: Yes. Toggle audio on/off in MaxVideoAI, and write voice/ambience/FX cues into your prompt.

faqs.q: Is it more expensive than Sora 2?
faqs.a: Yes—Sora 2 Pro is priced higher per second. Check the live rate in Generate before running a job.

faqs.q: Should I prototype in Sora 2 first?
faqs.a: Usually yes: explore cheap and fast in Sora 2, then regenerate your selects in Pro at 720p/1080p on the current MaxVideoAI route with audio control.

faqs.q: Can I do image-to-video with Sora 2 Pro?
faqs.a: Yes. Upload a still (PNG/JPG/WEBP/GIF/AVIF) up to ~50 MB, then describe motion and audio per beat.

custom.relatedSubtitle: Compare pricing, latency, and output options across other engines available in MaxVideoAI.
custom.finalPara1: Sora 2 Pro in MaxVideoAI is built for premium, client-ready outputs with control over motion, audio, and continuity.
custom.finalPara2: Prototype in Sora 2, then step into Sora 2 Pro when it’s time to polish and deliver in the current higher-quality MaxVideoAI route.
custom.tipsIntro: Sora is most predictable when you keep the shot simple, readable, and physical.

promptStructure.title: Prompt Structure
promptStructure.quote: Scene 1 → Neon-lit bazaar at dusk, camera pushes through stalls, layered percussion builds → Scene 2 → Rooftop skyline reveal, voice-over continues narration.
promptStructure.description: Lay out each beat to keep multi-scene briefs coherent:
steps: Break prompts into Scene 1 / Scene 2 segments
Specify camera movement + duration per scene
Call out audio cues (voice, ambience, FX)
Mention what should stay consistent (characters, wardrobe)
Add transitions (match cuts, crossfade, whip pan, etc.)

tips.title: Tips & Tricks
items: 🔗 Use scene weights to emphasize key shots
🎬 Repeat character descriptors to maintain continuity
🎧 Write audio beats explicitly when chaining prompts
🖼️ Upload reference stills for lighting consistency
🚀 Clone jobs into Veo/Pika to compare motion styles

prompts.title: Multi-scene legend opener
prompts.prompt: Scene 1 → A medieval city under the stars, camera slowly pans above rooftops, lute music plays, voice-over: 'Peace reigned for a century.' → Scene 2 → A rider gallops across the plains at sunrise, orchestral swell, zoom-in to sword hilt glowing.
prompts.notes: Use multi-prompt chaining to stitch scenes together in a single render.

prompts.title: Torchlit discovery
prompts.prompt: Scene 1 → Interior of a jungle temple at dusk, drums echo in the background, voice-over: 'Legends speak of a guardian.' → Scene 2 → Torchlit hallway, camera glides toward ancient glyphs as the music builds.
prompts.notes: Start from a detailed concept still to keep glyphs and lighting consistent.

faqs.q: Does Sora 2 Pro always output audio?
faqs.a: Audio is on by default for lip-sync and sound design, but you can toggle it off in the composer if you only need visuals.

faqs.q: How is Sora 2 Pro priced in MaxVideoAI?
faqs.a: The page shows MaxVideoAI preset totals for common Pro scenarios. The Generate workspace remains the source of truth for the exact live quote before you run a job.

faqs.q: What is the max duration for Sora 2 Pro?
faqs.a: The current MaxVideoAI route exposes 4s, 8s and 12s generations. For longer edits, render multiple clips and stitch them in post.

faqs.q: What resolution does Sora 2 Pro support here?
faqs.a: The current MaxVideoAI route exposes 720p and 1080p outputs. Provider/model-family docs may describe broader capabilities, but this page reflects the route available in the app.

faqs.q: Can I use image-to-video with Sora 2 Pro?
faqs.a: Yes. Upload a still as the starting frame and describe the motion, camera and optional sound cues for the generated clip.

faqs.q: When should I use Sora 2 instead of Sora 2 Pro?
faqs.a: Use Sora 2 for cheaper concept passes and early exploration. Use Sora 2 Pro when a selected shot needs higher-quality review, stronger polish and the Pro route.

decisionCards.title: Pro or standard Sora 2?
decisionCards.body: Use standard Sora 2 for 720p concept passes. Use Pro when a winning shot needs 1080p output and more final-quality review.

decisionCards.title: Ready for final review?
decisionCards.body: Use Pro after the prompt, framing and audio cues are already close. That keeps higher-cost iterations focused.

decisionCards.title: Comparing premium finals?
decisionCards.body: Compare Sora 2 Pro with Veo 3.1 or Kling 3 Pro when selecting a final route for ads, explainers or cinematic inserts.

referenceWorkflows.title: Text-to-video final
referenceWorkflows.body: Use a compact director brief with one clear action, camera move and sound plan.

referenceWorkflows.title: Image-to-video final
referenceWorkflows.body: Start from an approved still when identity, product shape or framing must stay stable.

referenceWorkflows.title: Audio planning
referenceWorkflows.body: Separate voice, ambience and SFX so the sound brief does not fight the visual action.

referenceWorkflows.title: Continuity anchors
referenceWorkflows.body: Repeat wardrobe, props, location and lighting when a 12s clip contains several beats.

referenceWorkflows.title: Cost control
referenceWorkflows.body: Prototype in Sora 2, then reserve Pro for the shots worth polishing.

section.title: How to Write a Great Sora 2 Pro Prompt
section.intro: Sora 2 Pro works best when the brief separates the shot, the source image role, the synced audio plan and the final delivery constraints.
section.tip: Tip: duration, aspect ratio and resolution are set in the UI — your prompt controls subject, action, camera, lighting, style and sound.
section.referencesTitle: How Sora 2 Pro uses prompts, references and audio

tabs.label: Text
tabs.title: Text-to-video Pro brief
tabs.description: Use this when the shot starts from language only.
tabs.copy:
Duration / output:
[4s, 8s or 12s] • [16:9 or 9:16] • [720p or 1080p]

Subject:
[Who/what appears + 2 defining traits]

Action:
[One visible action, then one optional reaction]

Camera:
[Shot size + angle + one camera move]

Style / lighting:
[Production look + light source + palette]

Audio:
[Ambience + 1-2 SFX cues or one short spoken line]

Constraints:
No logos, no unreadable text overlays, no extra characters.

tabs.label: Image
tabs.title: Image-to-video from an approved still
tabs.description: Use this when the first frame, product shape or character identity must stay stable.
tabs.copy:
Source image role:
[Identity / product shape / composition / environment]

Motion:
[What changes after frame 1; keep it simple]

Camera:
[Static / push-in / dolly / handheld drift]

Continuity anchors:
[Wardrobe, product details, lighting, background]

Audio:
[Room tone, music bed, SFX, or short dialogue]

Final frame:
[Where the motion should land by the last second]

tabs.label: Audio
tabs.title: Synced audio prompt
tabs.description: Use this when sound timing is part of the decision.
tabs.copy:
Visual beats:
0-3s: [visible action]
3-8s: [reaction or reveal]
8-12s: [final beat]

Dialogue:
[One short line, if needed]

Ambience:
[Room tone / street bed / crowd / weather]

SFX:
[Tie each sound to a visible action]

Mix note:
[Keep voice clean / keep music low / no dialogue]

tabs.label: Final
tabs.title: Final delivery check
tabs.description: Use this before spending a higher-cost 1080p Pro pass.
tabs.copy:
Delivery target:
[Client review / ad insert / product hero / cinematic B-roll]

Approved direction:
[Prompt or image direction already validated in Sora 2]

Non-negotiables:
[Subject identity, prop, location, motion timing, audio cue]

Quality check:
[What must look clean at 1080p]

Avoid:
[Artifacts, extra limbs, fake logos, unintended text, off-beat audio]

globalPrinciples: Use visual anchors (specific nouns > vague adjectives)
Start from an image for maximum control
Iterate: change one thing at a time
engineWhy: Too many beats can cause drift — keep each clip to 2–3 clear actions.
Image-to-video locks the first frame composition; prompt controls motion and timing.
Reference image rules are strict — clean stills, no logos, no readable text.
Audio cues help pacing; describe 1–2 key sounds for rhythm.

demo.title: Demo: 8s Pro scene continuity check
demo.prompt:
Single CCTV-style 16:9 shot in an active construction area.
A crew has just poured a smooth wet cement layer. A cat crosses the frame at the wrong moment, leaving paw marks while workers react in the background.
Camera: fixed security-camera angle, slightly compressed lens, no dramatic push-in.
Style: believable site footage, overcast daylight, wet cement texture, practical safety cones and warning tape.
Audio: off for this render.
Format: 8s, 16:9.
notes: Matches the hero/gallery render metadata instead of reusing a generic prompt.
Use the same structure for Pro checks: one scene, one event, clear camera, clear output settings.

summary.subject: Cat crossing fresh cement
summary.action: The cat crosses the site while workers react
summary.style: Believable construction footage, overcast light, wet texture
